default_description: Approved SQLite database for read-only local-data questions. Call database_context_read to read schema and saved hints.
default_description_short: Approved SQLite database for read-only local-data questions.

user_actions.manage_needed: No approved databases are available. Ask the user to open manage_href.

recovery_guidance.database_query_unavailable_database_id: Call database_list_info to choose an available database_id.
recovery_guidance.database_context_read_unavailable_databa: Call database_list_info to choose an available database_id.
recovery_guidance.memory_database_hint_remember_unavailabl: Call database_list_info to choose an available database_id.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Messages conversations. Handles are phone/email identifiers; resolve names through Contacts or Recents.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Messages SMS/iMessage conversations. Handles are phone/email identifiers, not names. For full conversations, join chats through chat_handle_join and chat_message_join. Message dates use Apple epoch values. Call helper_datetime_to_iso8601 to display message dates. Read message text from message.text. Use attributedBody as fallback. Attachments join through message_attachment_join.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Contacts names, organizations, phone numbers, emails, addresses, groups, and notes.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Contacts address book. Use for contact questions. Use for resolving names, phone numbers, and email addresses used by Messages, Mail, Calendar, and Recents. Contact details commonly join ABPerson.ROWID to ABMultiValue.record_id. Sample property values before choosing joins.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Contacts thumbnails and full-size contact photos.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Contacts image database. Use for contact photos. Pair with AddressBook.sqlitedb for names and details.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Calendar events, calendars, attendees, organizers, alarms, notes, and locations.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Calendar events and metadata. start_date and end_date commonly use Apple seconds. Call helper_datetime_from_iso8601 to create date bounds. Call helper_datetime_to_iso8601 to display dates. Use Contacts for attendee and organizer identity.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Calendar supplemental reminder and notification settings.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Calendar supplemental reminder, notification, and acknowledged-alarm metadata. Use as support for Calendar.sqlitedb. Prefer Calendar.sqlitedb for event questions.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Mail mailbox, message date, flag, folder, and thread metadata.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Mail metadata index. For Inbox, identify the mailbox row first. Then filter messages by mailbox id. Flags may be typeless text. Query typeof() and sample values. Join to Protected Index by Envelope messages.ROWID -> Protected messages.message_id. date_received and date_sent are Unix seconds.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Mail senders, recipients, subjects, body fragments, and protected message data.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Mail protected content index. Use for senders, recipients, subjects, and bounded body snippets. Pair with Envelope Index for mailbox, dates, flags, and thread context. Inbox rows require an Envelope mailbox filter.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Mail recent email addresses, display names, sending addresses, and last-used dates.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Mail recent-address database. Use for autocomplete and recent-recipient questions. Use Contacts for full identity and contact details. Use Mail indexes for message contents.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Photos asset, album, filename, date, identifier, comment, and keyword metadata.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Photos metadata for assets, albums, filenames, dates, identifiers, comments, keywords, and sidecars. It does not contain original image or video bytes.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Photos supplemental model-interest and activity counters.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Photos supplemental activity and model-interest counters. Use as support for Photos.sqlite.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Photos change-tracking database for sync metadata.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Photos change-tracking database. Prefer Photos.sqlite for photo-library content questions.

database_descriptions.description_short: LINE Messenger account, contact, chat, message, read receipt, sticker, and media metadata.
database_descriptions.description: LINE Messenger data. Use for LINE chat, contact, and message questions. This database is self-contained and separate from native Apple databases.

database_descriptions.description_short: ENIL URL cache for cached HTTP data.
database_descriptions.description: ENIL URL cache. Use for cache/debug questions. Prefer enil.sqlite for messages, contacts, chats, and stickers.

database_descriptions.description_short: Reeder accounts, feeds, folders, articles, unread/starred state, and stream metadata.
database_descriptions.description: Reeder RSS metadata. Use for feed lists, article metadata, read/unread state, and starred-item questions. Pair with rkit-data.db for article body text.

database_descriptions.description_short: Reeder article titles, authors, links, extracted content, and readability content.
database_descriptions.description: Reeder article-content database. Use for article body text and extracted content. Pair with rkit.db for feed, folder, status, and date context.

database_descriptions.description_short: Reeder pending local edits and sync actions.
database_descriptions.description: Reeder edit/sync-queue database. Use for unsynced edit and local action diagnostics.

database_descriptions.description_short: Reeder backup users, accounts, streams, feeds, folders, and starred articles.
database_descriptions.description: Reeder backup database. Use for recovery and historical context. Prefer live rkit.db and rkit-data.db for current article questions.

database_descriptions.description_short: Reeder URL cache for cached HTTP requests, responses, receiver data, and blobs.
database_descriptions.description: Reeder URL cache. Use for cache/debug questions and cached response data. Prefer rkit.db and rkit-data.db for feed and article questions.
